# ADR-006: `js/data.json` canonical, `js/data.js` auto-generated

**Status:** Accepted
**Date:** 2026-06-10
**Issue:** [#102](https://github.com/Johnesco/karaokedirectory/issues/102)
**Related:** [ADR-002](002-vanilla-js-no-build.md) (no build step) · [ADR-005](005-venue-json-schema.md) (venue schema) · [ADR-004](004-parallel-data-source-flag.md) (parallel data source)
**Amends:** ADR-002 — carves out a narrow exception for data-only generation.

## Context

`js/data.js` historically served two purposes that pulled in opposite directions:

1. **Canonical storage** of all venue data — every dev script reaches into it.
2. **Browser runtime artifact** — loaded synchronously via `<script src="js/data.js">` so `karaokeData` is available globally in both `index.html` (consumed by `app.js`) and `submit.html` (consumed by the tag-grid populator).

The runtime requirement forces a JS wrapper. The storage role would be cleaner as pure JSON. Four scripts (`validate-data.js`, `audit-for-supabase.js`, `seed-from-data.js`, `geocode-venues.js`) all coped by parsing the JS file with the same regex (`/const\s+karaokeData\s*=\s*(\{[\s\S]*\})\s*;?\s*$/`) and feeding the captured group into `JSON.parse` (or worse — two of them used `eval()`). Any drift in formatting (trailing comma, comment, an `export const` instead of `const`) silently broke all four.

`geocode-venues.js` made it worse: it rewrote the entire file via `JSON.stringify(data, null, 2)`, destroying any whitespace or key-ordering the curator had set. Adding coordinates to one venue produced a massive whitespace diff.

ADR-005 introduced a real JSON Schema. Pure JSON unlocks `"$schema"` autocomplete in VSCode — but only if the file is `.json`.

## Decision

`js/data.json` is the canonical source of truth.

`js/data.js` is **derived**: a one-line wrapper `const karaokeData = ${JSON.stringify(data, null, 2)};` regenerated by `scripts/sync-data-js.js`. CI runs `node scripts/sync-data-js.js --check` and fails the build if `data.js` diverges from `data.json`.

### Why keep a JS wrapper at all?

Async data loading in `index.html` and `submit.html` would require non-trivial refactoring (top-level await in modules; submit's `populateTagCheckboxes` synchronously reads `karaokeData`). That work is out of scope for this ticket. The wrapper lets the runtime stay exactly as it is while the storage layer evolves.

### Why call out an amendment to ADR-002?

ADR-002 said "no build step." A 25-line node script that converts JSON to `const ... = ...;` is technically a build step. The carve-out is narrow:

- **Allowed:** scripts under `scripts/` that derive a data artifact from another data artifact, gated by `--check` in CI.
- **Still disallowed:** webpack/rollup/vite, TypeScript compilation, CSS preprocessing, JS bundling.

The substance of ADR-002 — "files served as-is, no toolchain to learn" — holds. A new contributor still serves the directory with `python -m http.server` and it works.

## Consequences

### Positive

- Dev scripts read JSON directly. The four regex parses and two `eval()` calls go away.
- VSCode autocompletes inside `data.json` once `"$schema"` is added (lands as a follow-up — needs `schema/venue.schema.json` from ADR-005 to exist).
- `geocode-venues.js` patches `data.json` and runs sync — no whole-file reflow disaster.
- Curator's build step (commit `bb82d73`) updates to emit `data.json` then call sync — one extra `node` invocation.
- CI gate catches drift: edit `data.json`, forget to sync, PR fails fast.

### Negative

- Two files now exist where there used to be one. Anyone editing `data.json` and forgetting to run sync hits the CI gate — annoying but not data-corrupting.
- ADR-002 is now amended; documentation must clarify "no build step" doesn't mean "no node scripts."

## Alternatives considered

- **Browser fetches `data.json` at runtime.** Cleanest long-term, but pushes async into `index.html` and especially `submit.html` (which synchronously reads `karaokeData` in `populateTagCheckboxes`). Saved for a follow-up if/when Supabase migration forces async anyway.
- **Keep `data.js` canonical, regenerate `data.json` from it.** Solves the regex-parse problem for scripts but doesn't unlock `$schema` autocomplete (the file with `$schema` has to be the one being edited). Reversed.
- **Embed `data.json` content as a `<script type="application/json">` inside an HTML page.** Avoids the wrapper, but moves the storage into HTML — uglier than the current setup.
- **Single file with both shapes.** Not possible without inventing a custom format.

## Related work

- **#99 / PR #103** introduced CI as the gate
- **#100 / PR #104** authored the venue schema
- **#101 / PR #105** aligned `submit.html` to the schema shape
- This ticket finishes the loop: the canonical artifact becomes JSON, and `submit.html`'s emit step matches what `data.json` stores
